rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.

rules_evaluated.evidence: 1:10 — Opens with Boris-direct platform truth (CTO tooling misconception); transitions through Roger Martin strategy-frame (systems-throughput question); Rory reframe embedded throughout (e.g., line 12: invert from 'faster pipes' to 'what are humans for'). Voice quintet deployed correctly for CTO boundary surface.
Fully composed prose, not a rough-cut scaffold.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 9:10, 17:18, 33:34, 53:57 — Curran (Intercom), Charles (Ramp), Klaassen (Every) all cited with URLs and [practitioner direct] labels. No technique borrowed without attribution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training creator's name banned in student-facing body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No creator name (Antti, etc.) in body. File is CTO-facing reference, not personal memo.
No author-we. Lines 31, 36 use second-person (you) or impersonal (teams, orgs). No "we the authors" framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 6, 31, 36, 37, 43 — proper second-person 'you' and team-collective framing. No 'the student' or trainer cues in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: auto-merge, verifier, eval, judges all operationally grounded in body prose with sufficient context. eval-of-evals (line 23) is named and explained inline.
No reassurance hedging detected. Line 43 asserts firmly ('you can run hot'), no softening.
No user-given verbal frames in this artifact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Supplementary (not module). Three anchor cases cited once each per section. No redundancy or credit-collecting.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 5-6 ('If you think... wrong room') and 39 ('Most orgs get this arrow backwards') both hold throughout their sections. No overclaim requiring carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: CTO-facing supplementary (explicit audience framing). No defensive anti-vendor or anti-consultant rhetoric. Reframe from tooling-focus to systems-thinking is substantive, not positioning callout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module 6 supplementary. All operational terms (agents, evals, verifiers, rules, loops) earned by M6. No premature jargon planting.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit the closer when trimming a body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Reference prose, not an exercise with forcing-function trim scenarios.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No combative verbs (bark, order, command, force, drag, push, etc.) applied to human-agent interaction. Line 21 'forced switch' refers to latency constraint, not agent coercion.
